mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth in the labor market indicates robust corporate hiring and consumer spending, which drives earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeds 100k but 5-day forward returns are negative in 60%+ of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3pp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Unemployment rate increases by 0.3pp but 20-day returns are positive in > 50% of cases, or IC < 0.01 in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and inflation expectations (CPI) signal accommodative policy and stable real rates, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both decline but 60-day returns are negative in > 40% of cases, or interaction term IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand-side momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: GDPC1 and HOUST both rise but 20-day returns are negative in > 30% of cases, or interaction term IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A 2%+ annualized increase in CPI (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, which depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL rises 2% but 5-day returns are positive in > 50% of cases, or IC < 0.01 in 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises but 20-day returns are negative in > 40% of cases, or IC < 0.02 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) signals a resilient demand environment, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns during post-recession recoveries.
failure_condition: RSAFS and UNRATE both show favorable trends but 20-day returns are negative in > 30% of cases, or interaction term IC < 0.02.
